_label_tier_note: AE Stage B cleanup: must_detect is the strict detector-aligned benchmark tier; ideal_must_detect preserves broader auto-labeled statutory coverage for gap discovery. ideal_must_detect is not a locked recall baseline. Stage B labels are project-owner approved for internal benchmarking. Current strict tier was realigned after UK/EU precision guards; removed strict spans are preserved in ideal_must_detect for gap review.

must_detect.matched_text: General notes: this is guidance, not legal advice; not all fields are mandatory; no Emirates ID is required for anonymous feedback; lower‑case pm note: move retention epic to sprint‑12 after QA; product refs: FalconTab F9, DesertWave M3 (fictional devices); see https://falajmarkets.example.ae/help?id=USR-7u8x9z&flow=retention.

DPL Transfer Workflow Notice (fictional controller, UAE context), issued under the internal compliance code FM-PDPL-24-07 with reference to non-public issuer information obligations in free-zone market rules (fictional).
Controller: Falaj Markets PJSC, trade licence TL-58 2093 114 (fictional), Level 14, Al Waha Tower, Capital District, Abu Dhabi 00000, UAE; Data Protection Officer: dpo@falajmarkets.example.ae; DSAR portal: https://portal.falajmarkets.example.ae/dsar?ticket=ab12cd34&sess=AZ-0091 (internal use).
Purposes and categories: we process Emirates ID copies (masked), client trade licen­ces, device identifiers, access logs, payroll account metadata, and limited family‑origin declarations for KYC only where mandated by UAE PDPL; MNPI is handled strictly within issuer deal workflows and not used for marketing.
For Ms. Laila Al‑Qasiri (fictional data subject): DOB 1990-03-28 (34), Senior Compliance Analyst at Palm Oasis Ventures LLC (fictional employer), device FalconTab F9 IMEI 35199-02-123456-7, last login from Dubai (Jumeirah 3), IPv4 10.24.56.7, employee ID P0V-8842, payroll account AE22 3890 0012 3456 7800 01 (fictional), session ref sess:ab12-34cd, Emirates ID 7 8 4 - 1 9 9 0 - 0x1234 5 - 5 (masked), internal profile https://intra.falajmarkets.example.ae/users/view?uid=UQ-LAQ-9081&src=onb.
Deal workflow note: MNPI (e.g., unannounced issuer budgets, order book color, or pre-pricing allocations) is sequestered in “Project Palmwind” data rooms and disclosed externally only upon issuer approval and market release on the Emirates Growth Exchange (EGX, fictional).
Cross-border transfers: processors in the GCC and EEA-equivalent locations may receive pseudonymized records under FSC-UAE (fictional regulator) Standard Contractual Clauses; no onward transfer to non-adequate states occurs without a transfer impact assessment in the Al Zahra Global Market (AZGM, fictional) template.
DSAR handling: submit requests at privacy@falajmarkets.example.ae or via the portal; identity checks may use masked Emirates ID and trade licence screenshots; generic descriptors like “analyst, mid-30s, Abu Dhabi” alone are not sufficient for identification under PDPL.
Security measures: encryption-at-rest, role-based access; we do not record MAC addresses; not a mac address: aa-bb-cc-zz-11-22 is a sample in documentation only; public FAQ links do not contain MNPI.
Retention: trade surveillance logs 5y; device telemetry 180d; Emirates ID images 0d (redacted on ingestion); HR payroll metadata 6y; marketing consents 2y; retention timers pause for ongoing FSC-UAE (fictional) investigations.
Pseudonymization examples: EID_token=EID:7 8 4 - 1 9 9 0 - 1 2 3 ? ? ? 5; acct_token=acc:****‑****‑****‑7800‑01; tra de l icence captured as TL‑58‑20 93‑1 14 with OCR‑borne spacing to minimize full-string exposure.
Unlinked weak identifiers (non-attributable alone): DOB 1979-11-09; Al Ain; employer “Coastline Data Ltd” (fictional); role “analyst”; device “DesertWave M3”; account suffix ‑9001; none of these are associated here with a named natural person.
Market-abuse caution: issuer inside information designated under FSC-UAE Market Abuse Circular 7/24 (fictional) is non-public until EGX disclosure; public-source references (newspapers, issuer websites, regulatory bulletins) are not MNPI when genuinely public.
Special category handling: family-origin fields, if collected for KYC, are minimized, not used for profiling, and never included in marketing segments; no biometric templates are processed in standard onboarding.
Contacts and complaints: DPO dpo@falajmarkets.example.ae; escalation to the Federal Data Office (fictional), PO Box 112233, Oasis City, UAE; support chat available at https://help.falajmarkets.example.ae/chat?flow=dpia&step=2.
General notes: this is guidance, not legal advice; not all fields are mandatory; no Emirates ID is required for anonymous feedback; lower‑case pm note: move retention epic to sprint‑12 after QA; product refs: FalconTab F9, DesertWave M3 (fictional devices); see https://falajmarkets.example.ae/help?id=USR-7u8x9z&flow=retention.

uncertain.reason: Provided as an unlinked example and not attributable to an identified person in context.
Unique user ID; unclear whether to classify as employee_id or customer_account_number under current rules.
Family origin may be sensitive in PDPL context but not explicitly enumerated in the allowed label set.
Family origin may imply sensitive personal data; label taxonomy lacks a direct rule for this.
